---
title: "Conversational AI as a Marketing Engine"
subtitle: "How support conversations become customer signals"
author: "Khyati Parihar"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    fig-width: 8
    fig-height: 4.8
    df-print: paged
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd

pink = "#ff4fa3"
deep_pink = "#8a155f"
soft_pink = "#ffd6ea"
light_pink = "#fff0f7"
ink = "#2b1020"
muted = "#6b5261"
```

<style>
:root {
  --pink: #ff4fa3;
  --deep-pink: #8a155f;
  --soft-pink: #ffd6ea;
  --light-pink: #fff0f7;
  --ink: #2b1020;
  --muted: #6b5261;
}

body { color: var(--ink); }

h1, h2, h3, h1.title {
  color: var(--deep-pink);
  letter-spacing: -0.02em;
}

.subtitle {
  color: var(--muted);
  font-size: 1.1rem;
}

.hero {
  padding: 2rem;
  border-radius: 28px;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--deep-pink), var(--pink));
  color: white;
  margin: 1.4rem 0 1.8rem 0;
  box-shadow: 0 18px 45px rgba(255, 79, 163, 0.22);
}

.hero h2 {
  color: white;
  margin-top: 0;
  font-size: 1.9rem;
}

.hero p {
  font-size: 1.08rem;
  margin-bottom: 0;
}

.card-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(220px, 1fr));
  gap: 1rem;
  margin: 1.2rem 0;
}

.card {
  padding: 1.15rem;
  border-radius: 22px;
  background: var(--light-pink);
  border: 1px solid var(--soft-pink);
  box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(138, 21, 95, 0.08);
}

.card p { margin: 0.2rem 0; }
.card strong { color: var(--deep-pink); }

.big-number {
  font-size: 2.2rem;
  font-weight: 800;
  color: var(--pink);
  line-height: 1;
  margin-bottom: 0.5rem !important;
}

.flow {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 0.7rem;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  margin: 1.4rem 0;
}

.step {
  padding: 0.9rem 1.1rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  background: white;
  border: 2px solid var(--soft-pink);
  color: var(--deep-pink);
  font-weight: 700;
  box-shadow: 0 6px 18px rgba(255, 79, 163, 0.12);
}

.arrow {
  color: var(--pink);
  font-size: 1.5rem;
  font-weight: 800;
}

.callout {
  border-left: 6px solid var(--pink);
  background: var(--light-pink);
  padding: 1rem 1.2rem;
  border-radius: 16px;
  margin: 1.3rem 0;
}

.small-note {
  color: var(--muted);
  font-size: 0.9rem;
}

.tag {
  display: inline-block;
  padding: 0.28rem 0.75rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  background: var(--soft-pink);
  color: var(--deep-pink);
  font-weight: 700;
  font-size: 0.86rem;
  margin: 0 0.35rem 0.35rem 0;
}

hr {
  border: 0;
  height: 1px;
  background: linear-gradient(90deg, transparent, var(--pink), transparent);
  margin: 2.2rem 0;
}
</style>

::: {.hero}
## From chatbot to customer intelligence layer

Conversational AI is no longer just a support tool. It captures intent, reduces friction, and shows marketers what customers are trying to say.
:::

<span class="tag">Conversational AI</span>
<span class="tag">Customer Experience</span>
<span class="tag">Marketing Analytics</span>
<span class="tag">Netomi Lens</span>

---

## The simple problem

::: {.card-grid}
::: {.card}
::: {.big-number}
1
:::

**Customers move fast**

They expect instant answers across chat, email, social, and apps.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.big-number}
2
:::

**Brands move slower**

Teams often work in separate systems with limited customer context.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.big-number}
3
:::

**Support becomes brand memory**

A slow support experience can become a negative marketing impression.
:::
:::

::: {.callout}
**Core idea:** Customer service is not separate from marketing. It is one of the most direct brand touchpoints.
:::

---

## What conversational AI actually does

::: {.flow}
::: {.step}
Customer asks
:::
::: {.arrow}
→
:::
::: {.step}
AI detects intent
:::
::: {.arrow}
→
:::
::: {.step}
Answer or action
:::
::: {.arrow}
→
:::
::: {.step}
Human handoff
:::
:::

::: {.card-grid}
::: {.card}
**Not just replies**

It understands the reason behind the message.
:::

::: {.card}
**Not fully automated**

Complex or emotional cases should move to a human agent.
:::

::: {.card}
**Not only support**

Each conversation becomes a signal for marketing and retention.
:::
:::

---

## My Netomi lens

At Netomi, the key lesson was simple: **AI support works best when it feels invisible**.

::: {.card-grid}
::: {.card}
**Fast**

Resolve common questions without making customers wait.
:::

::: {.card}
**Useful**

Connect the conversation to the right workflow or answer.
:::

::: {.card}
**Human-aware**

Escalate when the customer needs empathy, judgment, or exception handling.
:::
:::

---

## From support tickets to marketing signals

```{python}
signals = pd.DataFrame({
    "Signal": ["Purchase intent", "Churn risk", "Product confusion", "Retention moment", "Campaign feedback"],
    "Marketing value": [88, 82, 76, 72, 66]
})

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4.8))
ax.barh(signals["Signal"], signals["Marketing value"], color=pink)
ax.set_xlim(0, 100)
ax.set_xlabel("Illustrative marketing value")
ax.set_title("What support conversations can reveal", color=deep_pink, weight="bold")
ax.invert_yaxis()
ax.spines[["top", "right", "left"]].set_visible(False)
ax.grid(axis="x", alpha=0.18)
ax.tick_params(axis="y", length=0)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.small-note}
Values are illustrative, used to show relative marketing usefulness rather than measured performance.
:::

::: {.callout}
**Marketing shift:** Support data helps teams see intent, pain points, and retention risk before they show up in dashboards.
:::

---

## A simple customer journey

```{python}
journey = pd.DataFrame({
    "Stage": ["Question", "AI answer", "Action", "Human help", "Loyalty"],
    "Experience score": [42, 62, 74, 84, 91]
})

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4.8))
ax.plot(journey["Stage"], journey["Experience score"], marker="o", linewidth=3, color=deep_pink)
ax.fill_between(journey["Stage"], journey["Experience score"], [35] * len(journey), color=soft_pink, alpha=0.55)
ax.set_ylim(35, 100)
ax.set_ylabel("Illustrative customer experience score")
ax.set_title("Better conversations can improve the customer journey", color=deep_pink, weight="bold")
ax.spines[["top", "right"]].set_visible(False)
ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.18)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.flow}
::: {.step}
“Where is my order?”
:::
::: {.arrow}
→
:::
::: {.step}
Delivery update
:::
::: {.arrow}
→
:::
::: {.step}
Return option
:::
::: {.arrow}
→
:::
::: {.step}
Better trust
:::
:::

---

## Why marketers should care

::: {.card-grid}
::: {.card}
**Personalization**

Conversations reveal what the customer needs right now.
:::

::: {.card}
**Retention**

Frustration signals can be caught before customers leave.
:::

::: {.card}
**Product insight**

Repeated questions show what is confusing or missing.
:::

::: {.card}
**Brand trust**

Fast, smooth support becomes part of the brand experience.
:::
:::

---

## Final takeaway

::: {.hero}
## The best conversational AI does not feel like AI.

It feels fast, useful, and human. For marketing analytics, conversations can become a live signal layer for customer intent, loyalty, and growth.
:::

**To summarize:** Conversational AI turns messy customer conversations into clearer marketing decisions.
